info.name: Cart Checkout — User Flows
info.description: Validates the three user flows from requirements.md (Happy Path, Payment Failure + Retry, Duplicate Webhook Handling) plus a folder of edge cases that exercise the order/payment state-machine invariants. All ids are chained via collection variables (cartId, orderId, paymentId, providerPaymentId, paymentIdRetry, providerPaymentIdRetry) so each folder is runnable end-to-end with the Postman Collection Runner. Override {{baseUrl}} to point at any environment.

item.name: Flow 1 — Happy Path
item.description: Create cart → add items → checkout → start payment → mock provider confirms → order PAID.

exec: pm.test("cart is still OPEN after adding an item", function () {
pm.test("cart total reflects the added line", function () {
pm.test("cartId is preserved on the order", function () {
pm.test("orderId is preserved on the payment", function () {

item.name: Flow 2 — Payment Failure + Retry
item.description: Setup a fresh cart/order, fail the first payment via direct webhook, then start a second payment attempt and confirm it. Demonstrates Payment-per-attempt and the PAYMENT_FAILED → PENDING_PAYMENT retry transition.

exec: pm.test("retry payment status is INITIATED", function () {
pm.test("retry creates a NEW payment (different paymentId)", function () {
pm.test("order status is PAID after retry", function () {

item.name: Webhook on Terminal Payment 1 is Idempotent

exec: pm.test("status is 200 OK — failed payment is terminal and immutable", function () {

item.name: Get Order (still PAID — terminal payment 1 did not flip order)
Flow 3 — Duplicate Webhook (Idempotency)
item.description: A confirmed payment receives the SAME webhook twice. Both calls return 200 and the second call is a no-op — proving the system tolerates provider retries without corrupting order/payment state.

exec: pm.test("status is 200 OK on replay", function () {

item.name: Webhook with conflicting outcome (replay-as-FAILED) is no-op

exec: pm.test("status is 200 OK — terminal payment ignores conflicting outcome", function () {

item.name: Get Order (still PAID — conflicting outcome ignored)

exec: pm.test("order remains PAID despite FAILED replay", function () {

item.name: Edge Cases — State-Machine Invariants
item.description: Negative tests that exercise the order/payment state machine: locked-cart 409, in-flight payment 409, unknown order/payment 404. These rely on the artifacts produced by Flow 1 (cartId, orderId, paymentId) — run Flow 1 first.

item.name: Add Item to Locked Cart (expect 409 CART_LOCKED)
Start Payment on Paid Order (expect 409 INVALID_ORDER_TRANSITION)
Start Payment on Unknown Order (expect 404 NOT_FOUND)
Trigger Mock Provider with Unknown Id (expect 404 NOT_FOUND)
